Framework · free to use

Real estate AI playbook for brokerages and property companies.

Real estate AI in 2026 has matured past the AI staging novelty. The brokerages and property management companies seeing real returns are using AI on the boring high-volume work — listing copy, market reports, tenant communications, deal docs. This is that playbook.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy
Published May 2026 · More from the library
Where the wins are

Five workflows

1. Listing descriptions & marketing copy

Generate listing descriptions, social posts, email blasts from property details in minutes. Agent time recovered for high-value selling activities.

2. Buyer/seller communications

Drafts of update emails, status notes, offer follow-ups. Agent reviews and sends. Higher touchpoint frequency without higher time cost.

3. Market reports for clients

Personalized quarterly or monthly market reports for past clients (top source of repeat & referral business). Move from "never produced" to "every quarter" with AI.

4. Transaction documentation summaries

Summarize inspection reports, title docs, disclosure packages into client-friendly summaries. Faster client decisions.

5. Tenant communications (property management)

Maintenance request triage, routine tenant communications, lease renewal outreach. Massive volume work where AI assistance scales the property manager.

Where to be cautious

Workflows to defer or skip

  • Pricing recommendations. Use comparative market analysis tools, not LLMs. Pricing is too high-stakes for AI-only judgment.
  • Contract drafting from scratch. Use your forms; let AI fill them or summarize them. Do not let AI improvise legal documents.
  • AI-generated property photos beyond staging. Disclosure requirements vary by jurisdiction; honesty risks outpace photo aesthetics.
  • Auto-replies to tenant emergencies. Routing is fine; substantive responses to safety/maintenance urgent issues should be human.
Brokerage rollout

Adoption patterns

  • Top-producing agents first. Their adoption signals everyone else.
  • Workflow-specific training. Generic AI demos do not convert agents. "Here is how you get a listing description in 90 seconds" does.
  • Shared prompt library. Agents copy what works from each other faster than they invent new workflows.
  • Brokerage-level enterprise seats. Do not make agents pay for personal accounts. Provision properly.
Property management rollout

Different shape

  • Property manager + admin team train together. The workflow split matters; both need the same vocabulary.
  • Integration with property management software. The bigger ROI comes from AI summarizing and drafting around existing systems, not replacing them.
  • Tenant-facing communications need brand voice. Build a Project with brand voice guide and example correspondence.
  • Vendor communications. AI drafts for contractor scheduling and follow-up are an underrated workflow.
Budget

Typical numbers

  • Small brokerage (5-15 agents): \$5K-\$20K Year 1 including platform + light implementation.
  • Mid-sized brokerage (15-50 agents): \$15K-\$60K Year 1.
  • Property management company (\$5M-\$25M revenue): \$10K-\$50K Year 1.
